rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L. BALSAM VS. DSG DIRECT INC., A FLORIDA CORPOATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions, And For A Finding Of Contempt Of Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 14, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFF DANIEL BALSAM'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions, And For A Finding Of Contempt Of Court. Plaintiff's Motion for monetary sanctions is granted. Motion for a finding of Contempt is denied. Plaintiff must personally serve defendant with Notice of Request for contempt or may make a showing that Defendant is secreting itself to avoid service. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TODD CARPER VS. ELECTRONIC PLASTICS, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Order Terminating Sanctions Against Deft For Failure To Comply With Two Discovery Orders Of The Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 14, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Order Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant For Failure To Comply With Two Discovery Orders Of The Court. Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Defendant's request for reconsideration is granted in part. The Court reconsiders and reverses the portion of its 2/9/11 Order deeming admissions admitted. At the time of the hearing, the Court was not aware that responses had been served, albeit improperly. These new facts warrant reconsideration under CCP1008. The remainder of the 2/9/11 Order stands, as well as the 5/5/11 Order. If defendant Gardiner has not done so already, he is ordered to properly serve responses to all outstanding discovery and pay the monetary sanctions previously ordered within 15 days. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TODD CARPER VS. ELECTRONIC PLASTICS, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 14, 2011, line 13, PLAINTIFF TODD CARPER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MSJ/MSA IS IMPROPER WHERE THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEPT. OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS V. UI VIDEO STORE, INC. (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 1084, 1097. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON SEVERAL ISSUES INCLUDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS THE ALTER EGO OF ELECTRONIC PLASTIC, WHETHER THE CONTRACT WAS BREACHED, WHETHER MIST WAS CONVERTED (AND FOR HOW MUCH) AND WHETHER THERE WAS ANY FRAUD. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN S. KAO VS. UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Notices Of Deposition And Deposition Subpoenas, To Stay Depositions And For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 14, 2011, Line 4. PLAINTIFF JOHN KAO'S Notice Of Motion To Quash Notices Of Deposition And Deposition Subpoenas, To Stay Depositions And For Protective Order. Motion to quash notices of Deposition and Deposition Subpoenas is denied. The discovery sought is directly relevant to the claims placed at issue in this case by Plaintiff. The parties shall meet and confer and enter and appropriate protective Order to protect disclosure of this information. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LA SALLE BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. TROPHY PROPERTIES B10 DE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Leave To File Action Against Receivership
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 14, 2011, line 1, OTHER MARY WIEDERHOLD's Motion For Order Permitting Leave To File Action Against Receivership. MOTION FOR ORDER PERMITTING LEAVE TO FILE AN ACTION AGAINST THE RECEIVERSHIP IS GRANTED. IT WOULD BE INAPPROPRIATE FOR THE TENANT TO INTERVENE IN THIS CASE SO THE COURT IS GRANTING THIS MOTION TO ADD THE RECEIVER TO HER CASE. OSTROWSKI V. MILLER (1964) 226 CAL.APP.2D 79. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST M THAYER VS. FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 14, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST, 3RD, AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND PER CCP SECTION 430.10(c). DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST EXPLICITLY ALLEGE WHICH OF DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATIONS WERE FALSE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MAY REASSERT THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IF SHE CAN ADEQUATELY AMEND THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION. =(302/LMG)
